frozen_components: host neural network (MNIST classifier or Pythia-70M-deduped language model)
architecture.summary: The paper studies sparse autoencoders (SAEs) applied to neural network activations, focusing on how weight regularization (L1/L2 penalties on encoder and decoder weights) affects feature stability and interpretability. SAEs extract human-interpretable features from model activations; the system reads activations from a frozen host model and trains an encoder-decoder pair to reconstruct them sparsely. Weight regularization is added during SAE training to improve cross-seed consistency and steerability of learned features.

biblio.title: Stable and Steerable Sparse Autoencoders with Weight Regularization

claims.statement: L2 weight regularization combined with tied initialization and unit-norm decoder constraints dramatically increases cross-seed feature consistency on MNIST
For TopK SAEs trained on Pythia-70M-deduped, adding a small L2 weight penalty increased the fraction of features shared across three random seeds
L2 weight regularization roughly doubled steering success rates on TopK SAEs trained on Pythia-70M-deduped
L2 weight regularization left the mean of automated interpretability scores essentially unchanged

In the regularized setting, activation steering success becomes better predicted by auto-interpretability scores, suggesting that regularization can align text-based feature explanations with functional controllability
